14.reason: This line has multiple errors: 1) The scanf format reads x1, x2, y1, y2 but the input order is x1 y1 x2 y2, so coordinates are mismatched. 2) The increment n++ is used four times, causing n to increase by 4 per segment instead of 1, leading to array out-of-bounds and incorrect data storage. 3) The while loop with EOF will not terminate properly for the given input format (first line has number of segments), causing infinite loop or incorrect reading.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: The scanf format reads four integers but assigns them to a[n].x1, a[n].x2, a[n].y1, a[n].y2, incrementing n each time. reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The input format expects the first integer to be the number of segments N, followed by N lines of 4 integers each. This loop reads all integers sequentially into x1, x2, y1, y2 fields incorrectly, treating the count N as coordinate data and misaligning subsequent inputs. It also lacks a bound check for n, potentially causing buffer overflow.
